contract.name: v2/download/transactions.md
contract.description: Starts an asynchronous download job that produces a .zip archive for prime transactions and subawards. A successful request returns a status_url for polling job state and a file_url for downloading the archive once ready.

filters.description: Search filter object. Must be a non-empty object, but unknown keys are silently ignored and award_type_codes are injected when omitted.
filters.constraints: Required. Missing/null/non-object -> 400. Empty object {} -> 400 ('At least one filter is required.').

keywords.constraints: If provided, must be an array of non-null strings; each string must have length >= 3.

keyword.description: Deprecated singular keyword search term; accepted by the API and normalized to keywords.
keyword.constraints: If provided, must be a string with length >= 3 (enforced after normalization to keywords).

time_period.constraints: If provided, must be a non-empty array (empty -> 422; null -> 400). Objects with only start_date or only end_date were accepted; invalid date strings -> 400.

date_type.description: Date field selector; 'new_awards_only' was accepted in probes.

recipient_search_text.constraints: If provided, must be an array of strings (string input -> 400). Probes showed multiple entries are accepted.

award_type_codes.constraints: Optional; if omitted the backend injects a default list into download_request.filters.award_type_codes.

agencies.constraints: If provided, must be an array of objects. Omitting required keys (e.g., tier) can trigger 422 validation errors.

place_of_performance_locations.constraints: If provided, must be an array of location objects; missing required location fields (e.g., country) can trigger 422 validation errors.

recipient_locations.constraints: If provided, must be an array of location objects; probes showed objects with country + zip are accepted.

naics_codes.constraints: Optional object; probes showed require/exclude arrays are accepted.

psc_codes.constraints: Optional object; probes showed hierarchical 'require' arrays are accepted.

tas_codes.constraints: Optional object; probes showed hierarchical 'require' arrays are accepted.

treasury_account_components.constraints: Optional array of objects; probes showed objects with aid/main are accepted.

columns.constraints: Optional array. Not validated at POST time; some column lists can later cause the async job to fail (status_url returns status='failed' with a message containing 'Unknown columns').

file_format.description: File format for generated files inside the ZIP archive.
file_format.constraints: Optional enum ['csv','tsv','pstxt']. If omitted, download_request.file_format was 'csv' in probes. Invalid values -> 400.

limit.description: Row limit for the download job.
limit.constraints: Optional. If omitted, download_request.limit was 500000 in probes. Values above 500000 -> 422. Probes showed 0 and negative values are accepted at POST time (job completion behavior not verified).

status_url.description: URL to poll download job status (GET). Observed as https://api.usaspending.gov/api/v2/download/status?file_name=... .

file_name.description: Name of the .zip archive (also used as the status endpoint's file_name query parameter).

file_url.description: Fully-qualified download URL for the generated .zip archive (observed host: files.usaspending.gov).

download_request.description: Echo/normalization of the request used by the backend (may include injected defaults).

file_format.description: Effective file_format used by the backend.

filters.description: Effective filters after backend normalization (e.g., default award_type_codes injected).

body.detail: Filters parameter not provided as a dict

quirks: filters must be a non-empty object, but unknown filter keys are silently ignored and can still yield a 200 response (bypassing the empty-filter guard).
filters.keyword (singular) is accepted and validated like filters.keywords (min length >= 3 after normalization).
If filters.award_type_codes is omitted, the backend injects a default award_type_codes list into download_request.filters.
columns values are accepted at POST time without strict validation; some column lists later fail asynchronously (status_url shows status='failed' with an 'Unknown columns' message).
download_request.download_types values differ from the endpoint documentation: observed ['elasticsearch_sub_awards','elasticsearch_transactions'].
risks: The default limit is 500000; omitting limit can create large downloads and slower async jobs.
Unknown filter keys are silently ignored, which can hide typos and produce broader-than-expected datasets; unknown keys can also bypass the empty-filter guard.
Unknown/unsupported columns are not rejected at POST time and can cause downstream async job failure; callers must poll status_url and handle failures.
gaps: Successful async completion was not observed via the status endpoint; only status='failed' (Unknown columns) and 404 (job not found) were probed.
The full set of valid column identifiers for this endpoint was not enumerated; POST accepts arbitrary strings but some values fail during job processing.
The effects of limit <= 0 on generated output were not verified beyond POST-time acceptance (200).

probes.notes: pass - minimal keywords accepted; default award_type_codes added and limit defaults to 500000
fail - filters must include at least one criterion

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Filters parameter not provided as a dict"}

probes.notes: fail - filters field required and must be dict
fail - keyword strings shorter than 3 chars rejected
pass - deprecated filter: 'keyword' singular string is accepted
pass - unknown filters ignored and defaults returned
pass - POST accepts columns list (job later failed with Unknown columns)

response.bodyExcerpt: {"status":"failed","message":"InvalidParameterException was raised while attempting to process the DownloadJob:\nTraceback (most recent call last):\n  File \"/data-act/backend/usas...

probes.notes: pass - status endpoint shows status='failed' and a message containing 'Unknown columns' for this job

response.bodyExcerpt: {"detail":"Download job with filename DefinitelyNotARealFile_1900-01-01_H00M00S00000000.zip does not exist."}

probes.notes: fail - status endpoint returns 404 for unknown file_name

mismatches: Docs describe download_request.download_types as ['sub_awards','transactions'], but live responses return ['elasticsearch_sub_awards','elasticsearch_transactions'].
Docs show status_url as localhost and file_url as a relative /csv_downloads path, but live responses return fully-qualified HTTPS URLs (file_url on files.usaspending.gov).
Docs show download_request includes an 'agency' field (e.g., 'all'), but live responses observed in probes omitted 'agency'.
Endpoint docs define filters.recipient_search_text as a string, but live API requires an array (string input returns 400).
Shared filters docs state recipient_search_text must not exceed a length of 1 item, but probes showed arrays with multiple items are accepted.
gaps: Successful async completion was not observed via the status endpoint; only status='failed' (Unknown columns) and 404 (job not found) were probed.
The full set of valid column identifiers for this endpoint was not enumerated; POST accepts arbitrary strings but some values fail during job processing.
The effects of limit <= 0 on generated output were not verified beyond POST-time acceptance (200).
risks: The default limit is 500000; omitting limit can create large downloads and slower async jobs.
Unknown filter keys are silently ignored, which can hide typos and produce broader-than-expected datasets; unknown keys can also bypass the empty-filter guard.
Unknown/unsupported columns are not rejected at POST time and can cause downstream async job failure; callers must poll status_url and handle failures.
